content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

May 18, 2015

Robert Boco
Sr. Assistant City Attorney
456 W. Olive Avenue
Sunnyvale, CA 94086

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-15-084

Dear Mr. Boco:

This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of Sunnyvale Mayor Jim Griffith
regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the
Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of
interest or Section 1090. This letter is based only on the facts presented. The Fair Political Practices
Commission (the "Commission") does not act as a finder of fact when it renders advice. (In re
Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71.)

QUESTION

May Mayor Jim Griffith participate in and vote on governmental decisions relating to the
Peery Park Specific Plan (the "Plan") despite being an employee of Apple, a company that has
leased offices in the Plan area?

CONCLUSION

While there may be minimal indirect impacts on Apple's use of the leased property resulting
from decisions made in connection with the Plan, there is no indication that any of the decisions
will affect Apple to such an extent that it will change the price of the Apple's publicly traded stock.
Therefore, the Mayor does not have a conflict of interest in the decision.

FACTS

The purpose of the Peery Park Specific Plan is to guide future development of the existing
industrial business park. It addresses the type, location, intensity, and design of industrial and
commercial buildings, as well as transportation and infrastructure improvements. The Plan would
include land use changes to allow for redevelopment of under-utilized or under-developed industrial

_______________________
¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. A-15-084
Page No. 2

properties to provide new commercial, industrial, and research and development space for
technology-based business development. It would also support uses within defined activity centers,
such as mixed commercial and residential uses along San Aleso Avenue on the east side of
Mathilda Avenue.

The Specific Plan would include development policies, land use regulations, design
standards, a capital improvement program, and a financing program concisely within a single
document to define and guide development within the Project area over the next 10 to 20 years.

Mayor Griffith is employed as a Senior Software Engineer at Apple Inc. and is currently
working at their office headquarters in the adjacent city of Cupertino. Apple Inc. recently leased
additional office space at 505 N. Mathilda Avenue in Sunnyvale, which is a building located within
the proposed Peery Park Specific Plan area. Moreover, Mayor Griffith will soon be working at the
Apple offices located at 505 N. Mathilda Avenue in Sunnyvale.

You provided the following additional facts in your correspondence of May 14, 2015:

1. The Apple lease is for additional office space for employees to perform their general job
duties. There is no specific product launch contemplated at the Sunnyvale office location.

2. Apple has already entered into the lease and any activities related to the Peery Park
Specific Plan will not affect the actual use of the property.

ANALYSIS

Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or using
his or her position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a financial interest.
(Section 87103.) Interests from which a conflict of interest may arise are defined in Section 87103
and include the following:

• Any source of income, except gifts or loans by a commercial lending institution made in
the regular course of business on terms available to the public without regard to official
status, aggregating $500 or more in value provided or promised to, received by, the
public official within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is made.

• Any business entity in which the public official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management.

Foreseeability: A conflict of interest may arise only when the reasonably foreseeable
financial effect of a governmental decision on a public official's interests is material. The standard
for foreseeability differs depending on whether an interest is explicitly involved in the decision.
Under your facts, Apple is not explicitly involved in the Plan. Thus, as applied to Apple, Regulation
18701(b) provides: "In general, if the financial effect can be recognized as a realistic possibility and
more than hypothetical or theoretical, it is reasonably foreseeable. If the financial result cannot be
expected absent extraordinary circumstances not subject to the public official's control, it is not
reasonably foreseeable."

File No. A-15-084
Page No. 3


Materiality under Section 87103(c), sources of income: Regulation 18702.3(a)(4) provides the source is a business entity that will be financially affected under the standards as applied to a financial interest in Regulation 18702.1.

Materiality under Section 87103(d), business positions: Regulation 18702.1(b) provides the test for materiality: "the financial effect is material if a prudent person with sufficient information would find it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision's financial effect would contribute to a change in the price of the business entity's publicly traded stock . . .."

Apple's April 27, 2015 press release, "Apple Reports Record Second Quarter Results" stated:

"Apple® today announced financial results for its fiscal 2015 second quarter ended March 28, 2015. The Company posted quarterly revenue of $58 billion and quarterly net profit of $13.6 billion, or $2.33 per diluted share. These results compare to revenue of $45.6 billion and net profit of $10.2 billion, or $1.66 per diluted share, in the year-ago quarter. Gross margin was 40.8 percent compared to 39.3 percent in the year-ago quarter. International sales accounted for 69 percent of the quarter's revenue."

You stated that the Plan does not affect Apple's existing lease. Moreover, the Plan will not affect the actual use of the property that Apple leases. While there may be minimal indirect impacts on Apple's use of the leased property resulting from decisions made in connection with the Plan, there is no indication that any of the decisions are of a magnitude that will affect the price of Apple's publicly traded stock. Therefore, the Mayor does not have a conflict of interest in the decision.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Hyla P. Wagner
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division
